title: Epipactis hemsleyana
intro: Epipactis hemsleyana, also known as Hemsley's helleborine, is a species of orchid native to China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis hemsleyana include iridoids, phenylethanoid glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a decoction, which is used to treat respiratory and immune system-related disorders.
taxonomy: Epipactis hemsleyana is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Epipactis, which is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as helleborines. The genus Epipactis is further divided into several subgenera, and E. hemsleyana falls under the subgenus Epipactis. This orchid species is native to China, where it grows in woodland and mountainous regions. Its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Epidendroideae, Tribe: Neottieae, Subtribe: Limodorinae, Genus: Epipactis, Subgenus: Epipactis, Species: E. hemsleyana.
